creatures.name: Arcane Sphere Of Faces
creatures.description: Safeguard of the Witchbane Ruins' basement cellar. Mysteriously rare and highly unconventional.

creatures.name: Giant Bat
creatures.description: The Giant Bats of Limgrave are nocturnal creatures and often rest during the day, unless they are disturbed.
creatures.location: Near the cliffs on Western Limgrave.

creatures.name: Lesser Runebear
creatures.description: Feral, often massive bears with runic patterns on their chest. Most commonly found scavenging the realms of The Lands Between or fighting other wild creatures.

creatures.name: Abnormal Stone Cluster
creatures.description: Bizarre catterpillar-shaped beings formed of moss-covered spherical stones and a disturbing eye that looks like that of a human being. They are commonly found as dull groups of seemingly lifeless rocks.

creatures.name: Bladed Talon Eagle
creatures.description: The servants of Stormveil come in many shapes, including Eagles that have been enslaved and fitted with blades. Driving them to attack those who approach

creatures.name: Boar
creatures.description: The Boars of Limgrave are less aggressive than normal, but should not be assumed to be passive
creatures.location: in Forests of Limgrave and Stormhill

creatures.name: Burning Slug
creatures.description: Burning Slugs move slowly and are located in the open field.

creatures.name: Crab
creatures.description: Crabs thrive in the shallow ponds of Agheel Lake and the shores of Limgrave
creatures.location: Roaming the beaches of Western Limgrave.

creatures.name: Construct
creatures.description: An Enemy made by Enemies

creatures.name: Deer
creatures.description: Deer roam freely in the plains on Limgrave, now that hunters have all but disappeared
creatures.location: West Limgrave, South West Liurnia of The Lakes

creatures.name: Dog
creatures.description: Roaming through the fields of Limgrave, wild Dogs should not be assumed to be docile nor dull.

creatures.name: Giant Dragonfly
creatures.description: The Great Dragonflies of Limgrave buzz and harass all creatures in their vicinity

creatures.name: Direwolf
creatures.description: Found alone or in small packs, these bloodthirsty beasts stalk the Lands Between with predatory fervor.
creatures.location: Caria Manor, Liurnia of the Lakes

creatures.name: Eagle
creatures.description: Majestic Eagles can be spotted among the cliffs, even during these times.

creatures.name: Giant Crab
creatures.description: Giant Crabs crowd the shores and shallows, attacking any who approach by swinging their powerful claws and spewing forth high-pressure water.
creatures.location: in the watery areas of Agheel Lake and the beaches of Western Limgrave

creatures.name: Giant Land Octopus
creatures.description: Giant Land Octopuses are a dangerous foe for any daring to visit the beaches of Limgrave.

creatures.name: Giant Lobster
creatures.description: The Lands Between are rife with all manner of life forms, naturally evolving over long periods and adapting to the environment. One such creature is the Giant Lobster — a creature that evolved to grow to such enormous sizes in order to better defend itself against much deadlier threats.

creatures.name: Giant Poison Flower
creatures.description: Giant Poison Flowers sprout in unlikely places, and summon down the elements on those who approach them
creatures.location: within Post Town Remains in Limgrave.

creatures.name: Guillemot
creatures.description: The Guillemot on the beaches of Limgrave are docile animals, but they are easily spooked
creatures.location: Roaming the beaches of Western Limgrave.

creatures.name: Lesser Runebear
creatures.description: Feral, often massive bears with runic patterns on their chest. Most commonly found scavenging the realms of The Lands Between or fighting other wild creatures.

creatures.name: Lesser Red Wolf Of Radagon
creatures.description: Hostile, over-sized wolves majestically covered in scarlet fur.

creatures.name: Lesser Spider Hand
creatures.description: Lesser Spider Hands are horrific creatures, surpassed only by their more powerful, gigantic counterparts.
creatures.location: Caria Manor, Liurnia of the Lakes, Mountaintops of the Giants

creatures.name: Land Octopus
creatures.description: The peculiar Land Octopus is a rather small but dangerous creature
creatures.location: In the watery areas of Agheel Lake and the beaches of Western Limgrave

creatures.name: Owl
creatures.description: Owls can be resting on the cliffs of Stormhill, and would even seem friendly and unconcerned by those passing by
creatures.location: perched on cliffs around Limgrave and Stormhill

creatures.name: Poison Flower
creatures.description: Poison Flowers grow near caves and cellars in Limgrave

creatures.name: Land Squirt
creatures.description: Land Squirts are repulsive shallow water creatures that emit a poison mist to kill and devour those nearby

creatures.name: Rat
creatures.description: Giant rats plague the mines and caves of Limgrave, spreading diseases where they go
creatures.location: Limgrave Tunnels, Ailing Village, Tombsward Cave

creatures.name: Rabbitgaroo
creatures.description: A species unique to The Lands Between, as adorable as scared.
creatures.location: Limgrave, South West Liurnia of the Lakes,

creatures.name: Rotten Stray
creatures.description: Undead dogs haunt the depths of Limgrave's mines, attacking all those who dwell into the tunnels

creatures.name: Sheep
creatures.description: The sheep from Limgrave have long forgotten captivity, romaing the landscape freely instead.
creatures.location: in the pastures of Limgrave and Stormhill

creatures.name: Skeletal Slime
creatures.description: Skeletal slime drudges the shores and shallows of Limgrave, looking for prey to consume
creatures.location: in the watery areas of Agheel Lake and the beaches of Western Limgrave

creatures.name: Spirit Jellyfish
creatures.description: Spirit Jellyfish are passive creatures seeking to protect, unless angered

creatures.name: Teardrop Scarab
creatures.description: There are both Crimson and Cerulean Teardrop Scarabs. Defeat them to replenish the flasks that match their type.

creatures.name: Spider Hand
creatures.description: Spider Hands skitter about their environs, often clinging to walls to put the drop on unsuspecting trespassers. They are also capable of burrowing deep beneath the earth, violently bursting out of the ground to grab and crush their prey.
creatures.location: Caria Manor, Liurnia of the Lakes, Mountaintops of the Giants
drops: Somber Smithing Stone (2), Somber Smithing Stone (7)

creatures.name: Wild Mouflon
creatures.description: Wild Mouflons become hostile when threatened and fight their aggressors by charging forward and striking them with their large horns.

creatures.name: Turtle
creatures.description: Turtles are passive creatures almost driven to extinction by those seeking its meat, said to have curative properties

creatures.name: Wolf
creatures.description: Wolves hunt the Lands Between in packs, and all those traveling should beware of their dangerous pack leaders

creatures.name: Prawn Miner
creatures.description: Hostile over-developed creatures resembling large shrimps. Remarkably agile.
creatures.location: Sellia Crystal Tunnel, Caelid Waypoint Ruins

locations.name: Bridge Of Sacrifice
locations.description: A busy bridge swarming with iron-clad foes. Be prepared to engage in an array of melee, ranged and artillery attacks or attempt to push past them

locations.name: Church Of Elleh
locations.description: Church of Elleh is a ruined building that hides a Site of Grace. The rooftop has caved in and offers no protection from the elements, and weeds are overtaking the walls, but this doesn't stop merchants from seeking what little shelter is on offer

locations.name: Deathtouched Catacombs
locations.description: Catacombs filled with the stench of death, Skeletons dwell in the shadows

locations.name: Church Of Dragon Communion
locations.description: The dilapidated remains of the Church of Dragon Communion showcase the ruin that came to the land. But could a flame still linger at its altar??

locations.name: Coastal Cave
locations.description: Coastal Cave is a dark opening on the side of a cliff. Its mossy depths are overrun by hostile humanoid creatures, but those who brave it may find passage to new lands

locations.name: Dragon-burnt Ruins
locations.description: Dragon-Burnt Ruins are what remains of the housing area among Agheel Lake, long lost to the deadly attacks of the fierce Agheel Dragon

locations.name: Forlorn Hound Evergaol
locations.description: The southern Evergaol of Limgrave, before the entrance to Weeping Peninsula

locations.name: Fringefolk Hero's Grave
locations.description: Fingefolk Hero's Grave is a dark and secluded location within the Cave of Knowledge. The confused and aimless dwell within

locations.name: Fort Haight
locations.description: The overrun fort of Kenneth Haith, the alleged next ruler of Limgrave

locations.name: Divine Tower Of Limgrave
locations.description: Closed with a giant set of doors. It looks like you need some pieces to interact with the tower.

locations.name: Gatefront Ruins
locations.description: Gatefront Ruins is what remains of the now destroyed barracks near the fortification. Despite its poor state, it is a heavily guarded location by those loyal to Stormveil

locations.name: Limgrave Tunnels
locations.description: Limgrave Tunnels are a mine harvested for Glimstone. Its zealous miners don't dare venture into its hidden chambers, for fear of the hoarder Troll within

locations.name: Groveside Cave
locations.description: Groveside Cave is a small refuge for wildlife in Limgrave. Its proximity to water and ease of access make it a sought-after shelter against bad weather, but those who venture in don't seem to return

locations.name: Minor Erdtree (mistwood)
locations.description: Easily distiguished from the rest of the trees in Mistwood Forest.

locations.name: Highroad Cave
locations.description: A cave that houses a tomb, deep within. It is situated at the northmost part of Limgrave. It has been inhabited by a pack of Wolves, Land Octopuses, and Giant Bats

locations.name: Mistwood
locations.description: Mistwood is a Location in Elden Ring. The Mistwood region is located in the Eastern part of Limgrave. Tiered terrain, small beaches, and luscious forests occupied by Lesser Runebears are the defining features of this region.

locations.name: Mistwood Ruins
locations.description: Concealed by the thriving growth of vegetation, these ruins are kept out of sight by the veil of nature itself. But there's more to it than what you see

locations.name: Murkwater Catacombs
locations.description: An underground cemetery that is located at the north-east side of Agheel Lake in Limgrave. Once a place for peaceful rest for fallen heroes, it is now infested by goblins.

locations.name: Murkwater Cave
locations.description: A dark cave halfway through the Murkwater River

locations.name: Siofra River Well
locations.description: A mystical underground region filled with its own set of skies and stars. Beware, it is teeming with creatures big and small alike

locations.name: Smoldering Church
locations.description: The Last Church in Limgrave before reaching Caelid

locations.name: Stormgate
locations.description: The Stormgate is a heavily guarded area that marks the border within Limgrave and Stormhill. Only the foolhardy would rush such against such odds

locations.name: Stormfoot Catacombs
locations.description: Stormfoot Catacombs were meant as a restful place for the deceased, but its now overrun by Goblins. Its deepest secrets are secured by the Guardian of the Burial Tree

locations.name: Stormhill
locations.description: Stormhill is a Location in Elden Ring. Stormhill is the upper half area of Limgrave, between its central river and Stormveil.

locations.name: Stormhill Evergaol
locations.description: The Brave and Foolish alike dare venture into the Stormhill Evergaol and face its relentless knight in battle

locations.name: Stormhill Shack
locations.description: The Stormhill Shack is a dilapidated shelter formerly used to house hunters in the area. A Site of Grace offers a needed moment of rest for those traveling nearby

locations.name: Stranded Graveyard
locations.description: Stranded Graveyard is a dark and secluded location within the Cave of Knowledge. The confused and aimless dwell within

locations.name: Summonwater Village
locations.description: D, Hunter of The Dead, warns you of this nearby village taken over by death, known as Summonwater Village

locations.name: Third Church Of Marika
locations.description: The Ruins of an Old Church found in the Mistwood. The most prominent sight here is a statue of a lady with her arms outstretched

locations.name: Warmaster's Shack
locations.description: Small shack in a remote area near north-central Stormhill.

locations.name: Waypoint Ruins
locations.description: Waypoint Ruins consists of a group of broken buildings and abandoned campsites, now overrun with poisonous flowers and their crazed caretaker

locations.name: Ailing Village
locations.description: A small, abandoned village nestled within a forested area north of the Castle Morne Rampart. This village is host to long-dilapidated dwellings, inhabited only by hostile Demi-humans.

locations.name: Bridge Of Sacrifice
locations.description: A busy bridge swarming with iron-clad foes. Be prepared to engage in an array of melee, ranged and artillery attacks or attempt to push past them

locations.name: Callu Baptismal Church
locations.description: A dilapidated church, presumably frequented by the former residents of Ailing Village. Now it lies in ruin, its congregation replaced by a family of mutant vermin feasting on the remains of  unfortunate travelers

locations.name: Church Of Pilgrimage
locations.description: The Church of Pilgrimage is a broken building situated at the top of the cliff in the north of the Weeping Peninsula. Even with the walls and roof destroyed pilgrims still make the journey to visit this church

locations.name: Castle Morne
locations.description: Remants of a chaos swept castle, teeming with iron-clad enemies -now engulfed with fire, piles of remains and ruin.

locations.name: Demi-human Forest Ruins
locations.description: Demi-Humans congregate here to worship their queen. Defeating her will reward players with the Demi-Human Queen's Staff, which is excellent for beginner mages.

locations.name: Earthbore Cave
locations.description: Earthbore Cave is a small dark cave on the side of the Cliffside between Limgrave and Weeping Peninsula. One might be able to hear the squeaks of rats and the snoring of a large beast deep within if you listen closely

locations.name: Forest Lookout Tower
locations.description: A run-down tower built on the edge of the cliffs of the Weeping Peninsula, located west of the Bridge of Sacrifice.

locations.name: Impaler's Catacombs
locations.description: The Impaler's Catacombs are found in the east side of the Weeping Peninsula. Once used to store the bodies of the dead, it is now crawling with Imps.
